# 05 — Unsupervised processes: bare `spawn`, `GenServer.start`, and `Process.exit(:kill)`

**Severity:** Medium
**Location:** `lib/jido/agent_server.ex` (`start_signal_call_task/3`), `lib/jido/await.ex` (`all/3`, `any/3`, `kill_waiters/1`), `lib/jido/scheduler/job.ex` (`start/3`, `terminate/2`, `spawn_callback_worker/1`)

## Problem

Several places sidestep OTP supervision:

1. **`AgentServer.start_signal_call_task/3`** falls back to a bare `spawn(runner)` when
   the instance task supervisor isn't found. The runner executes user `cmd/2` code and
   plugin hooks completely outside the supervision tree — invisible to shutdown,
   observers, and `max_tasks` accounting.

2. **`Jido.Await.all/3` / `any/3`** spawn raw, unlinked waiter processes and later
   `Process.exit(pid, :kill)` them. Each waiter holds a `GenServer.call` into an agent;
   brutally killing them is mostly safe (call replies to a dead pid are dropped), but the
   waiters are unsupervised and unlinked to the caller, so if the *caller* dies, the
   waiters keep blocking agents' `await_completion` slots until their own timeouts fire
   (the agent does monitor the waiter pid — here, the spawned proxy, not the real caller,
   which also means `completion_waiters` tracks the wrong process for liveness purposes).

3. **`Scheduler.Job`** is started with `GenServer.start/2` — unlinked and unsupervised.
   Lifetime is managed manually via an owner monitor, and `terminate/2` kills its
   callback workers with `Process.exit(pid, :kill)`, even though those workers run user
   callbacks that may be mid-side-effect. `spawn_monitor` for the tick callback is again
   a bare spawn.

## Why it matters

"Let it crash" only works when crashes are observed by a supervisor. Bare-spawned
processes survive instance shutdown (or die silently), leak under error storms, and make
`:observer`/telemetry blind spots. `:kill` of user code mid-execution is the one exit
reason that can't be trapped or cleaned up after.

## Recommendation

- Remove the bare-spawn fallback in `start_signal_call_task/3`; if the task supervisor is
  missing, that's a configuration error worth surfacing (see finding 04), not something
  to paper over.
- In `Await`, use `Task.async/1` (linked, awaited) or `Task.Supervisor.async_nolink/2`
  against the caller-provided instance; shut waiters down with `Task.shutdown/2` rather
  than `:kill`.
- Run `Scheduler.Job` processes under a `DynamicSupervisor` per Jido instance
  (`Jido.scheduler_name/1` already exists but nothing is started under it), with
  `restart: :transient`. That would also let the per-agent exponential-backoff restart
  machinery in AgentServer (~150 lines of `cron_monitors` / `cron_restart_timer_refs`
  bookkeeping) shrink dramatically — supervisors already do this job.
title: F05: Unsupervised processes: bare `spawn`, `GenServer.start`, and `Process.exit(:kill)`
